svg.title: Daily cash activity notes display
svg.desc: The "Notes on Daily Cash Activity Report" document — the client's own explanation of the DCAR as the day's till tape, its two balancing sections, the manual staff-tip/purchase adjustments, and the weekly spreadsheet control — kept one tab away from the DCAR so reviewers always have the workflow's intent beside the numbers. Printed as its own sheet.
svg.text: Notes on Daily Cash Activity Report
Notes on Daily Cash Activity Report, (DCAR):

g.text: The DCAR is basically the 'till tape' of the day's transaction activity …
… divided into two sections: revenue and taxes retained, then balance
sheet adjustments where deposits are recognized and applied …
We are in the practice of making a few manual adjustments to reflect
staff tips and purchases plus any 'extraordinary' items …
The total of the top sections must balance with the lower section,
that lists all the payments we have received, by type …
… at the end of the week the daily reports are taken upstairs and
entered into a matching spreadsheet manually …

svg.text: The client's full wording is reproduced verbatim on the page.
